covered_rules: OWN-4 escape via store and via return
OWN-5 exclusivity (mut/mut, shared/mut, read/write/move under borrow)
DIAG-1 every rejection cites one rule ID (asserted in tests)
not_yet_covered: struct field types / real type checking
match/loop control flow interaction with liveness
finding: The D1a-simplified calculus (affine ownership + lexical regions + exclusivity, no inference) checks in ~230 lines with rule-ID-carrying diagnostics. This is strong directional evidence for frontend-scale effort: the owner condition. The hard remaining risks are rule soundness at scale (adopt a formalized calculus before ratification) and the not-yet-covered list, not code volume.
caveats: Toy AST, no parser/type checker integration; lexical liveness only (OWN-6) — rejection rates on realistic AI-written code unmeasured; place overlap conservative; single-author rules not yet cross-checked against Featherweight Rust/Austral formalizations.

soundness_bugs_found_by_critique_and_fix: OWN-4 outlives direction was INVERTED in the spec text (checker had the sound direction; spec text fixed) — found independently by two critics.

OWN-10 was missing entirely: returning/storing a borrow of an own binding into a caller-supplied region dangled. The v0 prototype ACCEPTED the dangling case (one positive test was the counterexample). Rule added to spec and checker; test flipped to negative.
Holder-rooted aliasing: places rooted at borrow-holder bindings did not resolve to the borrowed place, allowing invisible aliases of &mut content. resolve() added; OWN-5/7 now judge resolved places.
remaining_scope_gaps: copy/affine classification enforcement at expression level
v0_1_update.finding_update: Effort estimate unchanged (still frontend-scale: 262 lines after fixes), but the critique validated the ratification precondition: rule soundness, not code volume, is the risk. Two of three soundness bugs were in the SPEC TEXT while the checker was accidentally sound or unsound respectively — spec-CI tying rule text to checker test vectors (a critique fix suggestion) is now a required artifact.

newly_covered: OWN-11 loop back-edges (outer-region borrows and outer-binding moves rejected inside loops; region-per-iteration positive test)
OWN-12 call-argument aliasing (two uniq args or shared+uniq overlap rejected with rule-precise diagnostics; disjoint-field and shared+shared positives)
OWN-6 call-scoped temporaries (holderless borrows die at statement end)
